scope_note: QA Witt Tower ECG Ventricular-Flutter Orbit Discriminator cert [447]. Empirical cert applying the Witt tower three-tier orbit partition (MOD=27, T0=bins 0-8 Singularity neighbourhood, T1=bins 9-17 Satellite neighbourhood, T2=bins 18-26 Cosmos neighbourhood) to ECG recorded during ventricular flutter. Data: MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database record 207 (Moody & Mark 1983, doi:10.13026/C2F305, PhysioNet); MLII lead, 360 Hz. Signal feature: zero-crossing rate (ZCR) per 5-second window — a natural integer count (no float enters QA layer). Normal sinus rhythm epoch: samples 100000-400000 (277.8-1111.1 s, 166 windows). Ventricular flutter epoch: samples 554740-590149 (1540.9-1639.3 s, 19 windows, annotated VFL in MIT-BIH .atr file). All windows rank-normalised to Z/27Z across the combined pool of 185 windows. CERTIFIED FACTS: (C1) 166 normal + 19 VFL windows PASS. (C2) ALL 19 VFL windows excluded from T0; log10_p=-9.94 (under hypergeometric null) PASS. (C3) ALL 19/19 VFL windows in T2; log10_p=-10.10 PASS. (C4) Mean tier strictly increases: normal=0.880 < VFL=2.000 PASS. (C5) VFL tier set = {T2} only; VFL T0=0 T1=0; normal spans all three tiers PASS. (C6) Flutter ZCR ratio: VFL mean ZCR=37.63 / normal mean ZCR=18.07 = 2.08x >= 1.5 (certifies flutter oscillation frequency as the discriminating mechanism) PASS. ORBIT MAPPING: normal sinus rhythm spreads across T0 (37%)/T1 (37%)/T2 (25%) — QRS transients produce moderate ZCR; ventricular flutter 100% in T2 — continuous sinusoidal oscillations at ~3.8 Hz fundamental (225 bpm) produce maximal ZCR, placing all windows in Cosmos orbit. Theorem NT: ECG voltage (mV) is observer projection; ZCR integer counts are QA state; rank bins are integer arithmetic throughout. 6 checks PASS; 8/8 fixtures PASS. Live read via wfdb from PhysioNet mitdb; hardcoded fallback ZCR arrays included. PRIMARY SOURCES: Moody GB, Mark RG (1983) MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database, PhysioNet doi:10.13026/C2F305; Wall HS (1960) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Witt tower theory). Structural parent: cert [110]. Empirical chain extends certs [442]-[446]. Validated 2026-06-18.
